# Polygon Bridge

> Polygon Bridge covers self-custodied transfers of crypto assets from Ethereum to Polygon PoS — routes, fees, timing, and the risks worth checking first.

## Key facts
- Route: source chain  destination chain
- Deposit: typically minutes
- Withdraw: may need proving/finalization
- Gas: native asset on both chains
- Custody: Non-custodial — your wallet signs
- Verify: Use the official contracts; beware fake bridge sites

## On this page
- [What is Polygon Bridge?](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#what)
- [How bridging works](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#how)
- [Depositing to the destination](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#deposit)
- [Withdrawing back](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#withdraw)
- [Time and fees](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#fees)
- [Is Polygon Bridge safe?](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#safety)
- [Problems and fixes](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#troubleshoot)
- [FAQ](https://bridge-to-polygon.github.io/#faq)
